[
  {
    "idea": "Weekly Surge in Google Trends for Job Interview Preparation Searches",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "A notable increase in job interview preparation searches signals rising job market anxiety or new job seekers entering the market, often preceding increased hiring activity.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary benefits from increased employment and confidence in job market growth."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Google Trends interest for 'job interview preparation' rises by more than 15% week-over-week",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or when the trend drops below 5% week-over-week growth",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Labor market interest fluctuations frequently occur with monthly hiring cycles and seasonal job search trends."
  },
